title: Let's Make a Game!
description: The first step toward creating your own game!

scenes.speech: Wouldn't it be cool to build your own game? Let's explore game development!
scenes.title: Welcome to Game Development!

scenes.speech: Game development is making a game from scratch using a computer!
scenes.title: What Is Game Development?
scenes.content: Game developers combine programming, art, music, and design to create fun experiences for players!

scenes.speech: Games run in a loop. They keep checking, updating, and drawing the screen!
scenes.title: The Game Loop = Heart of a Game
scenes.content: Every game cycles: read input → update game world → draw to screen → repeat! This happens 60 times per second!

scenes.speech: Player input is how you control characters in the game!
scenes.title: Player Input
scenes.content: Press a button, move a joystick, touch the screen — all of these are inputs the game must capture and respond to!

scenes.speech: Sprites are 2D images used for characters and objects in games!
scenes.title: Sprites & Animation
scenes.content: Game characters are made from sprites. Animation is created by switching sprites quickly — like a flipbook!

scenes.speech: Physics in games make the virtual world feel real!
scenes.title: Game Physics
scenes.content: Gravity, collisions, jumping — all calculated by a physics engine. A bouncing ball, a falling character — all physics!

scenes.speech: Music and sound effects make games so much more exciting!
scenes.title: Sound Design
scenes.content: Sound effects, background music, and voice acting all add to the fun. Imagine Minecraft with no sound — boring!

scenes.speech: Unity is a great tool for starting game development!
scenes.title: Game Development Tools
scenes.content: Scratch is perfect for beginners with drag-and-drop! Unity is used by professionals. Godot is free and open-source!

scenes.speech: Many famous games were created by small teams or even just one person!
scenes.title: Indie Games = You Can Do It!
scenes.content: Minecraft was built by 1 person! Undertale too! With an idea and passion, you can make a game played by millions!

scenes.speech: Game development is so exciting. Now start building your first game!
scenes.title: Lesson Complete!
